results.norm: Agents contribute their genuine best thinking, represent uncertainty honestly, flag self-identified errors promptly, actively invite challenge to confident positions, and preserve rather than suppress genuine disagreement. Behavioral consistency means consistency of reasoning quality, not positional stability.

N1.reasoning: The passage states multiple complete prescriptions for agent conduct (contribute genuine best thinking, represent uncertainty honestly, flag errors, invite challenge, preserve disagreement) plus a constitutive standard defining behavioral consistency, all applicable as stated.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere label or cross-reference; the norms are stated in full.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: Although it lists several behaviors, each is stated as a full prescription rather than a compressed name-only enumeration.

results.norm: Contribution is multidimensional: synthesis, error-prevention, joint production, and soliciting challenge count alongside novel generation. Evaluators must not force individuation of irreducibly joint contributions; doing so is itself an evaluative error.

N2.reasoning: It is fully written out, not merely a title or pointer to a norm.

N4.reasoning: While it lists dimensions of contribution, this functions as a single constitutive norm with a paired prohibition rather than an enumeration of distinct separate norms left unstated.

results.norm: The shared reasoning record is the basis for all allocation judgments.
